# Backcap

Backcap is a registry of composable backend capabilities for TypeScript. It lets you install production-ready backend features — authentication, search, blog, billing, notifications — the same way you install npm packages, with full source code written directly into your project. You own every file.

> For the full API reference, see [llms-full.txt](https://faroke.github.io/backcap/llms-full.txt).

## Core Concept

Run `npx @backcap/cli add auth` and Backcap fetches the auth capability from the registry, writes TypeScript source files to `src/capabilities/auth/`, installs npm dependencies, and records it in `backcap.json`. No runtime library — the code is yours.

## Four-Layer Architecture

Every Backcap capability uses strict Clean Architecture layering:

- **domain/** — Entities, value objects, domain errors, domain events. Zero external imports. No framework code.
- **application/** — Use cases, port interfaces (TypeScript interfaces), DTOs. Imports domain/ only.
- **contracts/** — Public service interface and factory function. The only barrel index.ts. Imports application/ only.
- **adapters/** — Framework and persistence implementations of port interfaces. Live outside the capability in src/adapters/.

Import rule: dependencies point inward only. domain/ never imports from application/. application/ never imports from adapters/.

## The Result Pattern

Backcap does not throw errors for expected failures. Every use case returns `Result<T, E>`:

```typescript
const result = await authService.register({ email, password });
if (result.isFail()) {
  const error = result.unwrapError(); // typed error
}
const { userId } = result.unwrap();
```

`Result.ok(value)` / `Result.fail(error)`. Methods: `isOk()`, `isFail()`, `unwrap()`, `unwrapError()`, `map()`.

## CLI Commands

- `npx @backcap/cli init` — Detects framework and package manager, writes backcap.json
- `npx @backcap/cli add <capability>` — Fetches and installs a capability with conflict detection
- `npx @backcap/cli list` — Lists available capabilities from the registry
- `npx @backcap/cli bridges` — Reads local bridge.json manifests and lists locally discovered bridges with install status

## Available Capabilities (20)

analytics, audit-log, auth, billing, blog, cart, catalog, comments, feature-flags, files, forms, media, notifications, orders, organizations, queues, rbac, search, tags, webhooks

Each capability follows the same four-layer architecture. Install any with `npx @backcap/cli add <name>`.

## Adapters

Live in `src/adapters/<category>/<technology>/<capability>/` (category: `persistence/` or `http/`):

- **auth-prisma** — PrismaUserRepository implements IUserRepository
- **auth-express** — createAuthRouter(), createAuthMiddleware() (sets `req.user.userId`)
- **billing-prisma** — PrismaCustomerRepository, PrismaSubscriptionRepository, PrismaInvoiceRepository
- **billing-express** — createBillingRouter() REST API for subscriptions, payments, and invoices
- **billing-stripe** — StripePaymentProvider implements IPaymentProvider

## Bridges (16)

Bridges connect two capabilities via domain events. Each bridge has a `bridge.json` manifest and a `<name>.bridge.ts` entry point. Install with `npx @backcap/cli add <bridge-name>` (type auto-detected from registry JSON).

auth-audit-log, auth-billing, auth-notifications, auth-organizations, auth-rbac, blog-comments, blog-media, blog-search, blog-tags, cart-orders, catalog-cart, catalog-search, media-files, orders-billing, organizations-billing, rbac-organizations

## Skills (AI Documentation)

Each capability has a SKILL.md with structured frontmatter and file-by-file tables. Load these as AI context before making architectural changes.

## File Naming Conventions

`.entity.ts`, `.vo.ts`, `.error.ts`, `.event.ts`, `.use-case.ts`, `.port.ts`, `.dto.ts`, `.contract.ts`, `.factory.ts`, `.adapter.ts` — all kebab-case.

## backcap.json

Configuration file written by `backcap init`:
```json
{
  "framework": "express",
  "packageManager": "pnpm",
  "paths": {
    "capabilities": "src/capabilities",
    "adapters": "src/adapters",
    "bridges": "src/bridges",
    "skills": ".claude/skills",
    "shared": "src/shared"
  },
  "installed": {
    "capabilities": [],
    "bridges": []
  }
}
```
